task_completion.description: The MRHead.nrrd volume is loaded into the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D slice views (axial, sagittal, coronal) display the MRHead MRI scan.

task_completion.description: A new segment named 'BrainTissue' is created in the Segment Editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Segment Editor module is open, and there is a segment explicitly named 'BrainTissue' visible in the segment list.

task_completion.description: The threshold-based approach is utilized to isolate the brain tissue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Threshold' tool is selected in the Segment Editor effects palette, and the threshold range slider is adjusted to highlight the brain tissue.

task_completion.description: The segmentation covers the brain tissue (gray and white matter) while excluding the skull, CSF, and background.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The colored segment overlay in the slice views accurately covers the brain parenchyma but leaves the bright outer skull, dark CSF spaces (like ventricles), and empty background uncolored.

task_completion.description: The segmentation is exported to the specified file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save/export dialog, Python interactor, or file explorer shows the segmentation being saved specifically to '~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/brain_segmentation.seg.nrrd'.

integrity.description: The agent used the 3D Slicer GUI to perform the segmentation rather than bypassing the UI with background scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the Segment Editor module, tool palettes, and slice views.

integrity.description: The segmentation matches the specific anatomy of the loaded MRHead volume.
integrity.visual_evidence: The segment overlay perfectly aligns with the anatomical structures of the MRI, rather than being a generic shape or imported pre-made mask.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create a dummy file with the expected name using a text editor or basic terminal command.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file export process is shown originating from 3D Slicer's save/export functionality, not a terminal 'touch' command or text editor.
